mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate rises above a key threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy that increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative equity market performance as investors reprice risk.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from a cyclical low is a robust leading indicator of recession (the 'Sahm Rule'). This structural break in the labor market causes a sharp repricing of growth expectations, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp from its 12-month low
failure_condition: The signal triggers but the S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered periods during a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects a robust manufacturing and supply-side economy. This indicates strong corporate activity and profitability, supporting higher equity prices as earnings expectations are revised upwards.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is already tight (low unemployment), further monetary tightening by the central bank is perceived by markets as a high risk for policy error, potentially triggering a recession. This interaction of a strong labor market and hawkish policy leads to negative equity returns as risk premia rise.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 has positive 60-day forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in housing starts signals a rapid cooling in a key interest-rate sensitive sector of the economy. This serves as a leading indicator for broader economic weakness, leading to negative investor sentiment and short-term equity market declines.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to produce negative 5-day returns on average following a trigger event over a full business cycle (peak-to-peak).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, strong payroll growth is interpreted negatively by markets. It signals persistent wage pressures and forces the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance, increasing the probability of an economic hard landing and thus depressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: When CPI Y/Y is above 3%, instances of PAYEMS monthly change > 200k are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained deceleration in nominal retail sales growth indicates weakening consumer demand, which translates directly to lower corporate revenue expectations. This leads to negative forward equity returns as analysts downgrade earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month moving average of monthly RSAFS changes and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and negative over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP is the broadest measure of economic output. A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP growth signals a contraction in aggregate demand, leading to broad-based downward revisions of corporate earnings and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following the release of a negative quarterly GDP report, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 50% of the time.
